keyPoints: Defining characteristics of a DBMS: Data independence, Controlled parallelism, Data integrity, and Security
DBMS components: External data source, Database management system, and Application program
Types of DBMS: Hierarchical, Network, Relational, and Object-Oriented
DBMS advantages: Improved data security, Enhanced data integrity, and Increased data accessibility
DBMS disadvantages: High maintenance cost, Complex implementation, and Limited scalability
Database entities: Entities, Attributes, and Relationships
Database relationships: One-to-one, One-to-many, Many-to-many, and Composite
Database normalization: First normal form, Second normal form, Third normal form, and Boyce-Codd normal form
